behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: This behavior is about real STT model output fidelity, not just wiring. PROOF-BUNDLE: exact phrase: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels". Concrete miss at Integration: a stubbed transcript with perfect labels always passes, so it cannot catch diarization errors (merged speakers, dropped labels) that break attribution. MULTI-STEP check: rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets one model boundary (STT label preservation), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API (no mocked transcript), then assert speaker labels are present and stable enough for downstream attendee attribution on the produced transcript.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure boundary is parser logic for extracting structured fields from transcript text. No required OS/network/subprocess and no required model-quality judgment. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is "parsing extracts ... into the summary input structure" (pre-LLM deterministic transform). Rejected Integration because behavior does not require cross-module handoff to manifest.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parsing function with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real LLM decides to include in summary content. PROOF-BUNDLE: exact phrase: "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee". Concrete miss at Integration: mocked LLM responses can be forced to include all attendees, hiding real omissions or attribution failures in actual generation. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior describes one summary-generation decision outcome, not a required multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcripts with known attendee-task assignments into the real summarization call and assert the generated action-items list covers each assigned attendee at least once.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic routing/serialization logic once decisions are identified. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP not required for the stated boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text scopes to "logic routes ... into the `decisions` section schema" (mapping behavior), not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the routing/mapping function with pre-identified decision records and assert output populates `decisions` schema fields and excludes those records from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this checks whether the real model surfaces and places decisions correctly in generated structure. PROOF-BUNDLE: exact phrase: "Key decisions ... appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative." Concrete miss at Integration: deterministic mocked outputs can always place decisions correctly, so they cannot reveal real model failures to extract/locate decisions. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because text does not require validating multiple sequential agentic handoffs, only final model output quality/placement.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known decisions, run real summarization, and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: behavior explicitly requires sequential processing of multiple segments and merged final coherence, which is multiple agentic steps in sequence. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: not Integration because correctness depends on model outputs across segment boundaries; not just wiring. System concerns may exist, but the defining failure boundary is chained agentic processing + merge quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the production segmentation pipeline, verify sequential per-segment processing occurs, then validate merged output is one structured summary with cross-segment action items/decisions preserved coherently.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: this is explicit end-to-end execution over real input and includes multiple model-dependent stages. Lower levels cannot expose cross-stage failures (upload->transcribe->summarize->delivery) or intervention-required dead-ends. System is necessary but insufficient because the behavior boundary is full multi-step agent pipeline success.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting recording through upload, STT, summarization, and output delivery; assert final structured artifact is produced automatically with no human retries/patches between stages.
